senses.gloss: volume of a book or journal
to hit (someone) on the skin, to lash, to whip, to flog

examples.text: ٱَلزَّانِيَةُ وَٱلزَّانِي فَٱجْلِدُوا كُلَّ وَاحِدٍ مِنْهُمَا مِئَةَ جَلْدَةٍ
examples.english: The [unmarried] woman or [unmarried] man found guilty of sexual intercourse - lash each one of them with a hundred lashes …

senses.gloss: to be tough, to be hardy; to be steadfast
verbal noun of جَلَدَ (jalada, “to whip, to flog”) (form I)
verbal noun of جَلُدَ (jaluda, “to be tough, to be steadfast”) (form I)
verbal noun of جَلِدَ (jalida, “to be frozen; to freeze”) (form I)

etymology.text: From Proto-Semitic *gild-. Compare Aramaic 𐡂𐡋𐡃𐡀 (gldʾ), Classical Syriac ܓܠܕܐ (geldā), Jewish Babylonian Aramaic גלדא (geldā), Hebrew גֶּלֶד (géleḏ).
